title: agrostis canina subsp. pusilla
intro: Agrostis canina subsp. pusilla, also known as small bent or dwarf bent grass, is a subspecies of the Agrostis canina grass that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small size and compact growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Agrostis canina subsp. pusilla include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for natural remedies. Agrostis canina subsp. pusilla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
